title: BEM-918 v2 record

Purpose: update the BEM-918 working roadmap after operator remarks about the seven listed mechanisms/components and Telegram entry/exit route.
Operator remarks addressed:
- The previous document listed seven mechanisms without clearly marking which are core, support, legacy/rudiment, routing configuration or operator-gated.
- The updated document must clarify which components will actually be used in the new implementation and which are not primary.
- The updated document must confirm whether General Director and its Curator are the Telegram information entry/exit point.
Corrections included:
1. Component status model added: ACTIVE_CORE, ACTIVE_SUPPORT, LEGACY_COMPATIBILITY, ROUTING_CONFIG, OPERATOR_GATED, ARCHIVE.
2. ACTIVE_CORE: ai-devops-telegram-curator-webhook, POST / Telegram webhook, /codex-task and /codex-status, codex-runner.yml.
3. ACTIVE_SUPPORT: /autonomy endpoints, only through policy gate and trace/proof.
4. LEGACY_COMPATIBILITY: /gpt-safe-run and /gpt-safe-status, not primary path; issue #31 writes remain operator-gated.
5. ROUTING_CONFIG: Telegram target OBJ-GD-001 / EL-CUR-GD-001.
6. Telegram entry and exit route confirmed: Operator <-> Telegram webhook <-> EL-CUR-GD-001 <-> OBJ-GD-001 <-> Director/Worker through managed channel.
7. BEM-919 is updated to include component status inventory and removal/archiving of rudiments.
No repeated Worker review was requested by operator for this document revision.
